title: nlink-lab impair --show --json output
description: Emitted by `nlink-lab impair --show --json`. One row per endpoint declared in the topology. Endpoints with no qdisc installed (kernel default `noqueue`) appear as `null`. Numeric fields (delay_ms, jitter_ms, loss_pct, rate_bps) are present only when the corresponding netem parameter was set; absent fields mean 'no value', not 'zero'. The `partition` flag is true iff the endpoint is in partition/heal state — distinct from a user having installed `--loss 100%` directly.

endpoints.description: Map of `node:iface` endpoint string to its impairment row, or null if no qdisc is installed.

delay_ms.description: Mean delay in milliseconds. Omitted if not set.

jitter_ms.description: Jitter in milliseconds (the second value after `delay`). Omitted if not set.

loss_pct.description: Loss percentage in 0–100. Omitted if not set.

rate_bps.description: Rate in bits per second. Omitted if not set.

partition.description: True iff the endpoint is in partition state (its pre-partition impairment is saved off and a 100% loss qdisc is installed via `partition`/`heal`). False for direct `--loss 100%` installs.
